w:r.w:t: Next.js 14 • React • TypeScript • Supabase • Cloudinary • Tailwind CSS • shadcn/ui
Vercel • Resend • Giga Sans Typography • AI Chatbot • Past Question Bank

GRASAG-UPSA (Graduate Students' Association of Ghana – University of Professional Studies, Accra Chapter) is the official representative body for all postgraduate students enrolled at UPSA. This document provides a complete technical product specification for the design, development, and deployment of the GRASAG-UPSA digital platform.

The platform is a public-facing, content-rich website built with modern web technologies. It serves as the digital headquarters for graduate student services, academic resources, career development, event management, and student welfare. The only authenticated role is the Admin; all other content is publicly accessible.
Provide a comprehensive public portal representing GRASAG-UPSA to graduate students, faculty, employers, and the general public
Enable secure Admin content management via Supabase authentication
Deliver an AI-powered chatbot trained on UPSA (upsa.edu.gh) information to answer student queries 24/7
Host a structured Past Question Bank for postgraduate academic support
Publish and manage Graduate Opportunities: jobs, scholarships, fellowships, mentorship, and workshops
Deliver mobile-responsive, high-performance, SEO-optimized pages using Giga Sans typography and UPSA brand colors
Achieve enterprise-grade quality outcomes on a free-tier infrastructure stack
Dark Blue — RGB(0, 51, 102) | Hex #003366 — Matches UPSA branding
Gold — RGB(184, 134, 11) | Hex #B8860B — UPSA accent color
Dark Gray — RGB(51, 51, 51) | Hex #333333 — Maximum readability
Light Gray — RGB(200, 200, 200) | Hex #C8C8C8 — Clean, modern look
Giga Sans — applied globally across all website pages and components
Mobile-first, accessible, professional, and approachable; reflecting UPSA's Scholarship with Professionalism ethos
React framework, SSR/SSG, routing, API routes
Utility-first styling with custom UPSA tokens
AI chatbot powering UPSA Q&A assistant
Schema validation for forms and API

The GRASAG-UPSA platform is a Next.js 14 App Router monolith deployed on Vercel. It uses a server-component-first architecture, where data-fetching occurs on the server and interactive islands are handled by client components. This approach delivers optimal performance, SEO, and developer experience.
Server and Client Components, layouts, nested routes
Utility classes with custom UPSA design tokens; shadcn for accessible UI primitives
End-to-end type safety across DB, API, and UI layers
Primary data store for all entities; Row-Level Security policies
Admin-only JWT-based auth; SSR session management via middleware
Media upload, transformation, optimization, and CDN delivery
Conversational assistant trained via system prompt on UPSA content
CI/CD from GitHub; Edge network for global performance
Performance monitoring and user engagement tracking
The platform has two access levels only:
Access to all published website pages, AI chatbot, past question bank (browsing), and all public content including events, opportunities, news, leadership, and resources. No login required.

Full content management access via protected /admin dashboard. Authenticated via Supabase Auth (email/password + optional MFA). Can create, edit, delete, and publish all site content including news, events, opportunities, past questions, leadership profiles, and resource files.
Recommended monorepo layout for the Next.js project:
Next.js App Router root — all routes, layouts, pages
API Route Handlers — chatbot, uploads, subscriptions
Shared React components (UI, layout, chatbot, forms)
Utility functions, Supabase client helpers, Cloudinary config
TypeScript type definitions and Zod schemas
Global CSS, Tailwind config with UPSA tokens

All data is persisted in Supabase PostgreSQL. Row-Level Security (RLS) is enabled on every table. Public read policies are applied where content is public; write policies require the authenticated admin role.
Stores admin user profile data, linked to Supabase Auth users.
Leadership/Executive Council member profiles displayed publicly.
Controls display order on leadership page
News articles, press releases, and executive statements.
Graduate Opportunities: jobs, scholarships, fellowships, workshops, mentorship, and partnerships.
job | scholarship | fellowship | workshop | mentorship | partnership
Accounting & Finance | Law | IT | Consulting | etc.
Past Question Bank — uploaded past exam papers organized by programme and course.
MBA | MPhil | MSc | LLM | MA | PhD
Cloudinary URL for the uploaded PDF
Stores chatbot sessions for analytics and quality improvement.
Array of {role, content, timestamp} message objects
Hero, announcements, upcoming events, partner logos, stats banner
MBA, MPhil, MSc, LLM, MA, PhD
Full detail page for each listing
Exec contacts, office map, social media
Stats overview: published items, pending reviews, subscriber count, recent activity
Supabase Auth email/password form; redirects to dashboard on success
CRUD executive profiles; drag-to-reorder; Cloudinary photo upload
Rich text editor; draft/publish toggle; cover image upload; SEO fields
Event CRUD; date/time picker; cover image; featured flag; past/upcoming tabs
List/create/edit/delete all opportunity types with filters
PDF upload via Cloudinary; organize by programme and year
Logo upload; tier assignment; featured flag; ordering
View subscriber list; compose and send via Resend; archive
View conversation logs; monitor query themes; quality review
Site configuration, admin profile, password change
Receives user message; calls Anthropic Claude API with UPSA system prompt; returns streamed response
Validates email; inserts into newsletter_subscribers; sends confirmation email via Resend
Processes unsubscribe token; updates subscriber record
Receives file; uploads to Cloudinary; returns secure_url
Uploads PDF to Cloudinary raw storage; returns secure_url
Increments download_count; redirects to Cloudinary PDF URL
Receives anonymous form submission; stores in Supabase; notifies admin via email
On-demand ISR revalidation triggered after admin saves content
Dynamic Open Graph image generation for news/events/opportunities
Dynamic sitemap supplementing next-sitemap static output

The GRASAG-UPSA AI Chatbot is an embedded conversational assistant powered by the Anthropic Claude API. It is designed to answer questions from graduate students about UPSA policies, programmes, procedures, and GRASAG services. The chatbot reads from the upsa.edu.gh website content via a curated system prompt and contextual document injection.
Anthropic REST API called from /api/chatbot Next.js Route Handler
System prompt enriched with scraped/curated content from upsa.edu.gh (programmes, admissions, fees, contacts, calendar)
Streamed response using ReadableStream for real-time token display
Anonymous session_id stored in localStorage; message history passed per request
Floating chat bubble, fixed bottom-right, expandable panel with message history
Chat sessions optionally stored in chatbot_conversations Supabase table for analytics
Per-IP rate limiting on /api/chatbot: max 20 messages per hour via Vercel Edge middleware
If Claude API is unavailable, graceful fallback message directs student to upsa.edu.gh or GRASAG office
The chatbot system prompt is engineered to constrain the AI to UPSA-relevant topics and ensure accurate, helpful responses:
Identity instruction: "You are the GRASAG-UPSA Student Assistant, a helpful AI for graduate students at the University of Professional Studies, Accra (UPSA)."
Knowledge base injection: Key facts about UPSA programmes, admissions process, fees, academic calendar, graduate school contacts, and GRASAG services are included as structured context
Scope constraint: The assistant politely declines questions outside UPSA/GRASAG scope and redirects
Tone: Professional, warm, and concise — matching UPSA's Scholarship with Professionalism ethos
Citation instruction: When referencing UPSA website content, note that information may change and direct student to upsa.edu.gh for the latest details
Language: Responds primarily in English; acknowledges Ghanaian context naturally
What MBA programmes does UPSA offer? What are the entry requirements for MPhil?
How do I apply to the UPSA graduate school? What are the tuition fees?
When does the second semester start? When are exams?
How do I join GRASAG? What are the membership benefits?
Are there scholarships available? How do I find graduate jobs on this site?
What is the thesis submission process at UPSA? Where do I get formatting guidelines?
Who do I contact for a welfare issue? How do I access the health clinic?
When is the next GRASAG congress? How do I register for events?
Where can I find past exam questions for MBA Finance?
Where is the GRASAG office? How do I contact the Dean of Graduate Studies?
Position: Fixed, bottom-right corner; visible on all public pages
Trigger: Chat bubble with GRASAG logo icon and pulsing badge indicator
Panel: 380px wide, 520px tall; expands on mobile to full-screen overlay
Header: UPSA Student Assistant with GRASAG gold accent bar
Messages: Bubbles — user messages right-aligned (dark blue); AI responses left-aligned (white with gray border)
Input: Text field + send button; supports Enter key submission
Streaming: Typing indicator animation while response is being generated
Suggestion Chips: Initial greeting shows 4 quick-start suggestion prompts
Accessibility: ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus management

The Past Question Bank is a curated, publicly accessible library of past examination papers for UPSA postgraduate programmes. It is one of the highest-value academic support features of the platform, enabling graduate students to prepare effectively for their examinations.
Student visits /resources/past-questions from the navigation or homepage quick link
Filters questions by Programme (MBA, MPhil, MSc, LLM, MA, PhD), Course Name, Academic Year, or Semester
Browses the filterable, paginated list of available papers (cards showing title, programme, year, semester)
Clicks a paper card to view its detail page with full metadata
Downloads the PDF via the /api/past-questions/[id]/download endpoint (no login required)
Download count increments on each download for popularity tracking
Fills in: Title, Programme (dropdown), Course Name, Course Code (optional), Academic Year, Semester
Uploads PDF file — file is sent to Cloudinary via /api/upload/document and URL stored in Supabase
Sets is_published = true to make it visible publicly
Paper appears immediately on the public Past Question Bank page
Filter by programme, course name, year, and semester — all combinations supported
Full-text search across title and course_name fields via Supabase ilike query
Sort by most recent year, most downloaded, or alphabetical by course
12 papers per page with numbered pagination; infinite scroll option for mobile
Increments download_count per paper; most downloaded shown as popular badge
PDFs stored in Cloudinary raw bucket; accessed via signed temporary URLs for security
Admin preview before publishing; students see file size and upload date
Admin can upload multiple PDFs in batch with shared programme/year metadata
See Section 3.1 — Table: past_questions for full schema details. Key searchable fields: programme, course_name, academic_year, semester.
The homepage is the most critical page — it must communicate GRASAG-UPSA's identity, value, and core services within the first viewport.
Professional headshot, personalized welcome message, digital signature, GRASAG motto: THE NATION'S PREMIUM, three CTAs: Join GRASAG | Explore Graduate Opportunities | Latest News
Thesis Support | Events Calendar | Graduate Opportunities Board | Past Questions — icon cards with hover animation
Scrolling ticker of latest news headlines, registration deadlines, event reminders
4-card grid of current Executive Council members with photo, name, portfolio, link to profile
Horizontal scroll carousel; event card with date countdown, title, type badge, register button
3 featured opportunity cards (1 job, 1 scholarship, 1 fellowship) with deadline badges
Auto-scrolling logo carousel; link to full Partners page
Animated count-up: registered students | programmes | years of existence | partnerships
Banner promoting the UPSA Student Assistant chatbot with sample questions
Quick nav links | GRASAG + UPSA logos | social media | office address | newsletter
The strategic differentiator of the GRASAG-UPSA platform. Transforms the site from administrative portal to career accelerator.
Jobs & Internships | Scholarships | Fellowships | Workshops | Mentorship | Partnerships
Filter by type, industry, deadline (upcoming / this week / this month)
Client-side filtering with Supabase query params
Logo, title, organization, type badge, deadline indicator (red if ≤7 days), short description excerpt
Full description, requirements, deadline, application link or direct apply button, related opportunities
Accounting & Finance | Marketing | Law | IT | Oil & Gas | Consulting | Academia | Public Sector
Newsletter signup on opportunities page for job alerts
At /partners/join — employers submit opportunities via form; admin approves before publishing
Card grid with event image, date, type badge, location, countdown timer, register button
Full description, agenda, speakers (static), location map embed, RSVP/registration link
Grid with photo gallery thumbnails, event report PDF download link
Static content + event listing for congress; panels, research expo, job fair, awards night; travel info
Cloudinary-powered gallery with lightbox; organized by event
Paginated listing of all news and press releases
Professional photo cards for all current executives; name, portfolio, email, office hours; click for full profile
Dedicated page: full welcome address, president's vision, digital signature image, professional portrait
Bio, priorities, portfolio, welcome message, contact email, office hours
Table/grid of former executives by administration; tenure dates; legacy projects
GRASAG Constitution, membership forms, event flyers, congress proceedings, research templates
Membership form, event registration, welfare/grievance form, grant application, mentorship application
Code of conduct, election guidelines, anti-harassment, data privacy, financial transparency
UPSA website, SOGS portal, National GRASAG, ICAG, ACCA, CIM, CIMA, ICSA
Quarterly newsletter archive; subscription form; past editions as Cloudinary PDFs
Full filterable question paper library (see Section 6)
Admin visits /admin — middleware checks for valid Supabase session
Login page renders email/password form; calls supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()
On success, Supabase sets secure httpOnly cookie; middleware validates on subsequent requests
All /admin/* routes are protected by middleware: middleware.ts checks auth.getUser()
Admin can reset password via Supabase email reset flow
Session expires after 1 hour of inactivity; auto-refresh token extends active sessions
Stats cards (total news, events, opportunities, subscribers); recent activity feed; quick action buttons
Data table with search/filter; rich text editor (TipTap or Quill); draft/publish toggle; cover image upload; SEO title/description; URL slug auto-generation; delete with confirmation
SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on news
CRUD events; date/time picker; event type selector; cover image; featured flag; registration URL; past/upcoming filter tabs
CRUD all opportunity types; filter by type; deadline management; logo upload; publish/unpublish toggle
PDF upload via Cloudinary; metadata form; bulk upload support; published/draft toggle; download stats view
Drag-to-reorder executives; CRUD profiles; Cloudinary photo upload; current/past toggle; term dates
Upload files or enter links; category assignment; ordering; publish toggle
Logo upload; tier selector; featured flag; ordering; publish toggle
View subscribers (paginated); export CSV; compose newsletter using React Email; send via Resend API; archive sent newsletters
View recent conversations; filter by date; search by keyword; identify common question themes for system prompt improvement
Update site-wide meta (title, description, OG image); manage own admin profile; change password

Accepted: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max size: 5MB. Uploaded to Cloudinary via /api/upload/image. Auto-optimized to WebP with quality=80. Responsive srcset generated automatically by Cloudinary. Transformations applied: c_fill for thumbnails; w_1200 for hero images.
Accepted: PDF only. Max size: 20MB. Uploaded to Cloudinary raw storage. Accessible via signed temporary URL for public downloads. Admin can preview before publishing.
Accepted: JPEG, PNG, SVG, WebP. Max size: 2MB. Transformed to standard dimensions: 400x200 (landscape) or 200x200 (square). Background removed if PNG with transparency.
All files named with UUID prefix to prevent collisions: {uuid}_{original_filename}. Organized in Cloudinary folders: /grasag-upsa/images/, /grasag-upsa/documents/, /grasag-upsa/logos/.
Server Components fetch data at request time; no client-side waterfalls. Static pages (about, welfare, academics) are statically generated at build time with generateStaticParams.
News, events, and opportunity pages use ISR with revalidate = 3600 (1 hour). Admin triggers on-demand revalidation via /api/revalidate after publishing content.
All images served through Next.js <Image> component or Cloudinary CDN with responsive srcset. Lazy loading enabled by default. WebP format served to supported browsers.
Giga Sans hosted in /public/fonts. Loaded with font-display: swap. Preloaded in <head> for zero FOUT. Subset to Latin characters only to minimize file size.
Dynamic imports with next/dynamic for heavy components (Chatbot, rich text editor, Cloudinary upload widget). Code splitting per route.
Supabase responses cached with Next.js fetch cache tags. Cloudinary CDN handles all media caching with long TTL. Vercel Edge CDN caches static pages globally.
LCP < 2.5s | FID < 100ms | CLS < 0.1 | TTFB < 600ms. Monitored via Vercel Web Vitals dashboard.
Next.js 14 Metadata API used for all pages. Dynamic metadata generated from Supabase content for news, events, and opportunity pages.
Dynamic OG images generated via /api/og route using @vercel/og for news articles, events, and opportunities. Branded with UPSA colors and GRASAG logo.
next-sitemap generates static sitemap.xml at build time. /api/sitemap supplements with dynamic content URLs (news slugs, event slugs, opportunity slugs).
JSON-LD schema markup: Organization for GRASAG-UPSA; Event schema for all events; Article schema for news; BreadcrumbList for all pages.
Canonical tags on all pages to prevent duplicate content issues.
Generated by next-sitemap. Blocks /admin/* from crawling. Allows all public routes.
Auto-generated from content excerpt for news/events. Manually set for static pages. 155-character limit enforced in admin CMS.
WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance target across all pages
All interactive elements have visible focus indicators styled with UPSA gold (#B8860B) outline
ARIA labels on all icon buttons, form inputs, and navigation elements
Color contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 for all text/background combinations
Skip-to-content link at top of all pages for keyboard navigation
All images have descriptive alt attributes; decorative images use alt=""
Chatbot panel is keyboard navigable with logical tab order and Escape key to close
shadcn/ui components include built-in Radix UI accessibility primitives
Screen reader tested with NVDA (Windows) and VoiceOver (macOS/iOS)
Supabase project URL (public, safe to expose)
Supabase anon/public key (public, safe to expose)
Service role key — server-only, never expose to client
API key for Claude chatbot — server-only
Cloudinary cloud name — public for upload widget
Cloudinary API key — server-only for signed uploads
Cloudinary API secret — server-only, never expose
From address for outbound emails (e.g., hello@grasag-upsa.org)
Base URL for canonical links and OG images
Secret header for protecting cron/webhook endpoints
Branch → Vercel Environment: main → Production | develop → Preview. Preview URLs generated per pull request.
next build — generates static pages, runs ISR configurations, and creates server functions
Vercel Serverless Functions for API routes; Edge Runtime for middleware; Static Assets on CDN
GitHub Actions or Vercel's native CI: lint → type-check → build → deploy. Failing builds block production deployment.
Connect custom domain (e.g., grasag-upsa.org) via Vercel DNS or external registrar CNAME. Vercel auto-provisions and renews SSL certificates.
middleware.ts runs on Vercel Edge: protects /admin/* routes; applies rate limiting to /api/chatbot; handles Supabase session refresh on every request.
Vercel Analytics enabled; Web Vitals tracking enabled; Speed Insights panel in Vercel dashboard.
Vercel status checks; Supabase dashboard for DB health; Cloudinary usage dashboard; Resend delivery stats.
Upgrade to Pro ($20/mo) if >100GB or team collaboration needed
500MB DB storage; 5GB bandwidth; 50MB file storage; 50,000 MAU
Upgrade to Pro ($25/mo) if DB exceeds 500MB or >50K users
Ample for images and PDFs at GRASAG scale
Upgrade if media library grows beyond 25GB
Upgrade to Starter ($20/mo) if newsletter grows beyond 3K subscribers
Free credits via console; then PAYG billing
Moderate chatbot usage; bursty during exam periods
Monitor token usage; set spending limits in Anthropic console
Run the following commands to initialize the Next.js project with the required stack:
Create a new Supabase project at supabase.com
Run the database migration SQL (from Section 3) in the Supabase SQL Editor
Enable Row-Level Security on all tables
Create RLS policies as specified in Section 3.2
Enable Supabase Auth with Email provider
Create the first admin user via Supabase Authentication dashboard
Add the admin user's UUID to the profiles table with role = 'admin'
Copy SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to .env.local
Install Supabase CLI: npm install -g supabase. Use supabase gen types typescript for type safety
Create a Cloudinary account at cloudinary.com
Create two upload presets: grasag_images (image type, auto quality) and grasag_documents (raw type for PDFs)
Create folder structure in Cloudinary Media Library: /grasag-upsa/images/, /grasag-upsa/documents/, /grasag-upsa/logos/
Configure signed uploads for the admin panel; unsigned uploads for low-risk public assets if needed
Giga Sans is applied as the global font across all pages and components:
Obtain Giga Sans font files (.woff2 and .woff for optimal browser support)
Define @font-face rules in /styles/globals.css for all required weights (400, 500, 600, 700)
Configure font-family in tailwind.config.ts: fontFamily: { sans: ['Giga Sans', 'Arial', 'sans-serif'] }
Apply font-sans class to the <body> element in app/layout.tsx for global application
Use font-display: swap to prevent invisible text during font loading
Obtain Anthropic API key from console.anthropic.com
Craft the system prompt with UPSA contextual knowledge (curate key facts from upsa.edu.gh)
Enable streaming: use Anthropic SDK stream() method and return ReadableStream response
Build the ChatbotWidget React client component with useState for messages, useRef for scroll-to-bottom
Add the <ChatbotWidget /> component to app/layout.tsx so it appears on all public pages
Implement rate limiting in middleware.ts using IP-based request counting
Optionally log conversations to chatbot_conversations Supabase table for analytics
Sticky top navigation with GRASAG logo, full dropdown menu, mobile hamburger
Site footer with quick links, social media, newsletter signup, UPSA/GRASAG logos
Collapsible sidebar for admin dashboard navigation
Admin topbar with breadcrumb, notification bell, admin avatar/logout
Reusable page hero with title, subtitle, and optional breadcrumb
Wrapper with consistent vertical padding and max-width container
President's welcome, photo, CTA buttons, GRASAG motto
Event card with image, date badge, countdown, type badge, register CTA
Opportunity card with logo, title, deadline indicator, type badge
News article card with cover image, category badge, excerpt, read more
Executive profile card with photo, name, portfolio, contact link
Partner logo in auto-scrolling carousel on homepage
Animated count-up numbers for the statistics banner section
Past question paper card with metadata and download button
Reusable filter row with dropdown/tag filters for opportunities and past questions
Floating AI chatbot bubble and panel; client-side streaming integration
Email subscription form with Zod validation and API submission
Horizontally scrolling announcement bar below navbar
Reusable table with search, pagination, sort, and row actions (edit/delete/publish)
TipTap-powered WYSIWYG editor for news and event content
Cloudinary upload widget for image fields with preview and clear button
PDF upload component with file size display and Cloudinary integration
Toggle switch for is_published field with optimistic UI update
Composable form field wrapping React Hook Form + shadcn/ui Input + Zod error
Dashboard metric card with icon, label, count, and trend indicator
Supabase Auth JWT-based sessions with secure httpOnly cookies. Session managed via Supabase SSR package. Middleware validates session on every /admin request server-side.
RLS enabled on all Supabase tables. Public read policies restricted to is_published = true records. Write operations require authenticated admin session. Service role key never exposed to client.
All admin-facing API routes (/api/upload/*, /api/newsletter/send) validate Supabase session using supabase.auth.getUser() before processing. Unauthenticated requests receive 401.
All secrets (API keys, service role key) stored as Vercel environment variables. NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix only for client-safe variables. No secrets committed to Git repository.

All form submissions validated with Zod schemas on both client and server. SQL injection prevented by Supabase SDK parameterized queries. File upload validation: MIME type and size limits enforced server-side.
Next.js App Router Server Actions include built-in CSRF protection. Custom API routes validate Origin header for state-changing operations.
Vercel Edge Middleware applies IP-based rate limiting on /api/chatbot (20 req/hr) and /api/newsletter/subscribe (5 req/hr). Prevents abuse and protects API spending.
CSP headers configured in next.config.js to prevent XSS. Allows Cloudinary CDN for media; Supabase for API calls; Anthropic API for chatbot.
Vercel auto-provisions and renews Let's Encrypt SSL certificates. HSTS headers enforced. All traffic redirected to HTTPS.
Signed uploads used for admin document uploads. Cloudinary access control rules prevent unauthorized direct uploads. Media library organized by folder with access restrictions.
Next.js API routes are stateless Vercel Serverless Functions. Automatically scale horizontally on demand with zero configuration.
middleware.ts runs on Vercel's Edge Network in 30+ global regions. Auth checks and rate limiting occur close to the user with minimal latency.
High-traffic public pages (homepage, about, welfare) are statically generated and served from Vercel's CDN. Zero database load for these pages.
News, events, and opportunity pages use ISR — cached and served as static HTML with 1-hour stale-while-revalidate. Database queries run only on revalidation, not per user request.
Supabase uses PgBouncer connection pooling. Next.js server components use the @supabase/ssr client which reuses connections per request lifecycle.
All media served from Cloudinary's global CDN. Image transformations cached at edge. No load on Next.js servers for media delivery.
Three-tier caching: Vercel CDN (static assets + ISR pages) → Next.js fetch cache (Supabase queries) → Browser cache (images, fonts). Minimizes repeated database calls.
PostgreSQL indexes on frequently queried columns: is_published, type, programme, event_date, published_at, slug. Ensures fast queries even as data grows.
Primary headings, navbar background, admin sidebar, key UI elements
Sub-headings, CTA buttons, active states, borders, link hover
Table row alternating bg, section highlights
Feature callout boxes, alert banners, highlight cards
Table borders, card borders, dividers, input borders
Page background, card backgrounds, white text on dark bg
Alternating table rows, form input backgrounds
max-w-7xl (1280px) centered with px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 horizontal padding
py-16 (64px) for major sections; py-10 (40px) for sub-sections
p-6 (24px) for standard cards; p-4 (16px) for compact cards
Homepage cards: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3; Executive grid: grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4; Partners carousel: flex with gap
Buttons: rounded-lg (8px); Cards: rounded-xl (12px); Avatars: rounded-full; Badges: rounded-full
Cards: shadow-md; Navbar: shadow-sm; Admin sidebar: shadow-lg; Chatbot panel: shadow-xl
Complete all items before going live:
Supabase: All tables created with RLS enabled and policies configured
Supabase: First admin user created and profile record inserted
Cloudinary: Upload presets configured; folder structure created
Resend: Domain verified; FROM email configured and tested
Anthropic: API key obtained; chatbot system prompt reviewed and tested
Environment variables: All .env.local variables added to Vercel project settings
Giga Sans: Font files added to /public/fonts; Tailwind config updated
Vercel: Project connected to GitHub repository; production deployment successful
Custom Domain: Domain connected and SSL certificate active in Vercel
Admin Panel: All CRUD operations tested for each content type
Chatbot: 20+ test queries run; responses reviewed for accuracy
Past Questions: At least 10 past papers uploaded as seed data
Leadership: All current executive profiles created with photos
Homepage: President's message, executive spotlight, upcoming events, partners populated
SEO: All static page meta descriptions set; OG images tested
sitemap.xml: Generated and submitted to Google Search Console
Performance: Lighthouse score ≥90 on all key pages
Mobile: Full responsive test on iOS Safari and Android Chrome
Analytics: Vercel Analytics enabled; first-party data collection confirmed
Newsletter: Test subscription email sent and received successfully
Rate Limiting: Chatbot and newsletter API rate limiting confirmed active
Error pages: Custom 404 and 500 pages styled with UPSA branding
GRASAG-UPSA DIGITAL PLATFORM | TECHNICAL DOCUMENTATION v1.0 | JUNE 2026
